report_name: Anthropic's 'Mythos' AI Model Triggers Global Cybersecurity Overhaul

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Client Execution
attack_patterns.description: The Claude Mythos AI model was reported to be capable of chaining exploits to achieve complex objectives, including gaining initial access which can involve client execution.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The Claude Mythos AI model was confirmed to be able to execute multi-stage attacks, including escalating privileges after gaining initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Develop Capabilities
attack_patterns.description: The Claude Mythos AI model can autonomously generate functional exploit code for unknown vulnerabilities, effectively developing its own offensive capabilities.

attack_patterns.name: Adversarial AI Attacks
attack_patterns.description: Claude Mythos is an AI model capable of autonomously developing its own adversarial attacks by analyzing source code, finding vulnerabilities, and generating novel exploit code.

tools.name: Claude Mythos
tools.description: An unreleased frontier AI model from Anthropic capable of autonomously discovering and exploiting unknown zero-day vulnerabilities in major software. It is not being publicly released due to its potential for misuse.

identities.name: Anthropic
identities.description: A U.S. AI startup that developed the Claude Mythos model and announced its capabilities. The company has committed to not releasing the model publicly.

identities.name: UK AI Safety Institute
identities.description: A UK government agency that independently confirmed the offensive cyber capabilities of the Claude Mythos AI model.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: A major technology corporation given controlled access to the Claude Mythos model under 'Project Glasswing' to test its defenses.

identities.name: Goldman Sachs
identities.description: A major financial services corporation given controlled access to the Claude Mythos model under 'Project Glasswing' to test its defenses.

identities.name: India's finance ministry
identities.description: A government body in India assessing the risk of AI-driven threats like Mythos to its legacy banking software.

identities.name: China's cybersecurity industry
identities.description: The collective cybersecurity industry in China, which is reportedly seeing a surge in investment in anticipation of an AI-driven cyber arms race following the Mythos announcement.

identities.name: Qi An Xin
identities.description: A Chinese cybersecurity company mentioned as part of the industry reacting to the Mythos announcement.

identities.name: Sangfor Technologies
identities.description: A Chinese cybersecurity company mentioned as part of the industry reacting to the Mythos announcement.
